programmes.show_description: Hard-hitting Australian drama series. Ferguson puts her life on the line in an attempt to win over Kaz. Maxine is worried about a lump in her breast.
Noel is acting very strangely, turning his back on work and family. Could a move to Australia be the tonic he needs? Plus, Luke's relationship with sister Chloe comes under strain.
Noel attempts to get James and Josh off their computers, and Sophie is taken on a trip to European party city Amsterdam in an attempt to take her mind off getting older.
Will either Paul or Jessica be tempted by a statue of Hermes, a vintage pallet truck, or a fishing display case? And whose choices will be proved wisest in the final auction? (Ep32)

Matt performs an emergency C-section at the Ellis' farm; a French bulldog puppy has a large lump on his eye; Julian meets a unique sheep.

programmes.show_name: Somerset: Wonder of the West Country
programmes.show_description: A visit to Cheddar's famous gorge reveals how its world-renowned cheese is made - and the depths its makers go to get the flavour right! Plus, a trip to Dunster Castle. (Ep2)

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben travels to Ibiza to discover why a former merchant sailor set up a commune in a wild, secluded valley in the heart of this holiday party island.
Lifting the lid on the Luftwaffe, the revolutionary German air force created to annihilate the enemy from the skies.
Crime writer and amateur sleuth Jessica Fletcher is invited to the desert town of Gila Junction by a friend who suspects it is the site of dangerous chemical experiments.
A haunted spirit and antique smugglers are just a few challenges Jessica faces on her Irish vacation.

programmes.show_name: Columbo: Last Salute to the Commodore
programmes.show_description: Detective drama starring Peter Falk and Robert Vaughn. When a tycoon is found dead, Columbo thinks he has a straightforward case on his hands. (1976)
Recapturing the golden age of luxury train travel. A trip through the Bavarian Alps with stopping points including a former Olympic village and Germany's highest mountain.
A technological masterpiece lost to the sea. Once cutting-edge in design and construction, now lying disused, contaminated and dangerous. But who built it? And why was it abandoned?
In the Battle of Berlin of 1945, 100,000 German civilians fight to the death. How did the Nazis motivate civilians to fight even when defeat was inevitable? The answer is propaganda.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben spends a week with a couple who left behind the comforts of America to become missionaries in the freezing tundra of Outer Mongolia.
Alex Polizzi helps couple Mark and Liz, who invested every penny of their savings to fund the Sea Air Bed & Breakfast, a seven-bedroom 1930s former hotel near Skegness.

programmes.show_name: Inside the Four Seasons: Park Lane
programmes.show_description: We meet some of the hotel's most VIP visitors and discover how staff ensure everyone has the perfect five star stay. And it's all hands on deck for the arrival of the hotel's founder.
A pair of cash-strapped friends go on two very different holidays with wealthy go getters - a static caravan park in Weston-super-Mare and a grand hotel, nestled in the Prosecco hills.
Australian drama series. Ferguson strengthens her alliance with Kaz by blaming Will for her rape and Bea as his lover. Maxine receives a devastating diagnosis.
Daniel's move to Australia is under threat and Aimee's hairdressing career could be over before it's even started. How will Noel and Sue help their kids thrive in the face of adversity?
The Radfords discover that Tillie is being bullied at school because of her disability, which may affect whether she goes through with an operation. Plus, Sue turns bestselling author.
The auctioneers choose between a bottle of vintage port destined for the bottle bank, a reproduction 17th century fireback, and a collection of silver spoons. Plus the big money star lot. (Ep33)

programmes.show_name: Somerset: Wonder of the West Country
programmes.show_description: Thousands of Jane Austen fans descend on upmarket Bath, spectacularly rugged Exmoor hosts a pony gathering, and a fancy farm shop attracts doughnut devotees. (Ep3)

In the Battle of Berlin of 1945, 100,000 German civilians fight to the death. How did the Nazis motivate civilians to fight even when defeat was inevitable? The answer is propaganda.
Jessica is trapped in the dungeon of an ancient Irish castle, but uses her ingenuity to get rescued and find the treasure and a murderer.
Jessica rewrites a movie beset with artistic and financial problems. When a stunt man dies, the director is charged with murder, but it's up to Jessica to find the real killer.

programmes.show_name: Columbo: Ransom for a Dead Man
programmes.show_description: Crime drama starring Peter Falk and Lee Grant. A lawyer comes up with a clever plan to murder her husband. (1971)
Bill Nighy narrates the stories of epic train journeys through stunning scenery and natural beauty. The New Zealand South Island line scales the snowy Southern Alps.
Exploring a highway in the jungle that leads nowhere, a fortress on the edge of the sea, forgotten bunkers in the Alps and a mysterious manmade island.
One of the most bold operations of WWII: the Nazi occupation and fortification of Norway as Hitler demands defences along the country's 1500-mile coastline to stop an Allied invasion.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben meets Tod Kershaw, a former engineer now living a primitive life as a scavenger in the Appalacian mountain forest.
James May uncovers the truth behind Sir Walter Raleigh's legendary rise - from his persuasive skills to his New World ventures - and reveals the dark legacy of his explorations. (Ep2/3)

programmes.show_name: Stonehenge: The Discovery with Dan Snow
programmes.show_description: Dan pieces together the story of Stonehenge, revealing the latest discoveries that are solving its many mysteries, including how 20-tonne stones were moved.
